data.title: NFLX: Fairly Valued Amid Slowing Subscriber Growth, Structural Headwinds, and Risky WBD Acquisition Plans
data.description:
Jeffrey Wlodarczak, an analyst from Pivotal Research, reiterated the Hold rating on Netflix. The associated price target was lowered to $95.00.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jeffrey Wlodarczak has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Netflix’s growth profile, valuation, and competitive environment. He notes that while the company’s recent quarter modestly exceeded expectations on revenue and operating income and guidance for 2026 aligns with his prior outlook, subscriber numbers came in meaningfully below his forecast, with growth driven more by price hikes than by user additions. This has led him to cut his long-term subscriber projections and to apply a lower terminal EBITDA multiple in his valuation model, which together reduce his year‑end 2026 target price to $95 and limit upside from current levels.

At the same time, he sees structural pressures mounting as younger audiences increasingly favor short-form, free social platforms and as free ad-supported TV offerings gain traction among more price-sensitive households, both of which could weigh on Netflix’s long‑form streaming model over time. He also views Netflix’s planned, high-cost acquisition of WBD’s studio and streaming assets as a double-edged sword: it could solidify Netflix’s global leadership in premium content but introduces regulatory, execution, and pricing risks, along with the potential for a prolonged closing period and a higher ultimate purchase price. When combined with sizable content obligations, uncertainties around the ad-supported transition, reduced transparency from the cessation of subscriber reporting, and broader regulatory and M&A risks, these factors support his view that the stock is fairly valued, justifying a Hold rather than a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – PerPlexity also downgraded the stock to a Hold with a $96.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating on Datadog: Solid Near-Term Beat Potential, Strengthening AI-Driven Growth, and Attractive Valuation Entry Point
data.description:
Analyst Andrew Sherman from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Datadog and decreased the price target to $200.00 from $235.00.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Sherman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to both near-term upside and attractive longer-term positioning. He expects Datadog to deliver results above its already solid fourth-quarter guidance, supported by a recent trend of consistent outperformance versus management’s targets and a notable reacceleration in core usage growth. Sherman also views the company’s upcoming fiscal 2026 revenue outlook as likely to be set at a level that balances management’s conservative history with still-healthy demand, particularly as its AI-native customer cohort is expanding rapidly and becoming a more meaningful revenue contributor. In his view, concerns about competitive pressures are overstated, especially given positive signals from customer behavior and recent contract renewals.

In addition, Sherman highlights Datadog’s expanding sales force and strong sales rep retention as key drivers that should sustain robust growth into 2026, even as the company moves past an historically slower seasonal quarter. His proprietary sales headcount tracking shows continued investment in go-to-market capacity, which he believes will translate into higher productivity and revenue over the next several years. Survey work on IT budgets further supports this outlook, as a large majority of respondents plan to increase spending with Datadog over the next twelve months. With the stock trading roughly 40% below its prior highs and at about 27x EV/FCF, Sherman sees the current valuation as an appealing entry point relative to the company’s growth prospects, reinforcing his Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, Bernstein also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $180.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
